name: Guayas (Ecuador)
description: Five equal horizontal stripes of light blue and white alternating, beginning and ending with light blue, charged with three white five-pointed stars on the central white area.
history: Guayas, established on 25 June 1824 with capital Guayaquil, is Ecuador's most populous province. Its flag derives from the colours of the Guayaquil city flag (light blue and white) of the 1820 Guayaquil independence revolution; the three stars represent its principal cantons.

features.symbolism: alternating light-blue and white stripes, the colours of the historic Guayaquil flag

features.role: three white five-pointed stars on the central stripe
